title: Avoid Using Weak Hashing Algorithms for Security
description: Cryptographic hash algorithms such as MD2, MD4, MD5, MD6, HAVAL-128, DSA (which uses SHA-1), RIPEMD, RIPEMD-128, RIPEMD-160, SHA-1, HMAC-MD5, or HMAC-SHA1 are no longer considered secure due to the possibility of collisions. Safer alternatives, such as SHA-256, SHA-512, SHA-3, bcrypt, scrypt, argon2, or pbkdf2 are recommended, especially for password hashing.
recommendation: Use safer alternatives like SHA-256, SHA-512, SHA-3, bcrypt, scrypt, argon2, or pbkdf2.
impact: Using weak hashing algorithms can lead to security vulnerabilities.
